models.name: actlabel_sumtotal
models.description: This table serves as a lookup or dictionary for all possible learning activity types. It defines the distinct categories (e.g., 'eLearning Course', 'Instructor-Led Training') that an activity can be classified as within the system. Use this table when you need to: 1. Categorize and filter learning activities by type, 2. Generate activity type reports and analytics, 3. Configure different learning paths based on activity categories, 4. Display activity type icons and labels in the UI, 5. Set up automated workflows based on activity types, 6. Create learning catalogs organized by activity categories.

properties.description: The unique primary key for an activity type. This identifier is referenced by the 'activitylabelfk' in the main activity table to categorize each learning activity.
The official, user-facing name of the activity type or category (e.g., 'Virtual Classroom', 'Assessment', 'Webinar').
A detailed description of the activity type, explaining its purpose or what it represents.
A reference to a graphical icon used in the user interface to visually represent the activity type.
The exact date and time when the activity type was first defined in the system.

This table serves as a lookup or dictionary for all possible learning activity types. It defines the distinct categories (e.g., 'eLearning Course', 'Instructor-Led Training') that an activity can be classified as within the system.
queryPatterns: Find activity labels by actlabel_pk or actlabel_name
Search for activity types by description or icon
Filter labels by creation or modification date
Identify activity types used in specific learning activities (join tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal)
useCases: Learning Catalog Categorization: Organize and categorize learning activities for easy navigation and filtering
Reporting: Generate reports on learning activity distribution by type
Content Management: Standardize activity type definitions across the learning platform
UI/UX Enhancement: Display activity type icons and labels in the user interface
Workflow Automation: Set up automated workflows based on activity categories
